title: OFAC designates 35 entities in Iran shadow banking and shadow fleet
summary: OFAC sanctioned 35 individuals and entities tied to Iran's shadow banking infrastructure plus a Chinese oil refinery, 40 shipping firms operating Iran's shadow fleet, and 14 procurement networks tied to Shahed drone and missile component supply, surpassing 1,000 Iran-related designations since February 2025.
contents:
# OFAC designates 35 entities in Iran shadow banking and shadow fleet expansion

## Designation Scope
The Office of Foreign Assets Control announced fresh designations against 35 individuals and entities supporting Iran's shadow banking architecture, alongside a named Chinese oil refinery accused of processing sanctioned Iranian crude. The action also blocks 40 shipping firms operating vessels in Iran's dark-fleet rotation and 14 entities linked to procurement of components for Shahed-series drones and ballistic missile parts. With this round, OFAC has now issued more than 1,000 Iran-related designations since February 2025.

## Network Architecture Exposed
Treasury identified UAE-, Hong Kong- and Turkey-domiciled front companies serving as nominee buyers and trade-based money-laundering vehicles. The designated Chinese refinery purchased Iranian crude through false-flag transshipments after ship-to-ship transfers in Malaysian and Singaporean waters. Procurement networks routed precision components through Belarus, Armenia and the UAE before delivery to Iranian missile production sites.

## Enforcement Direction
Treasury characterised the action as part of a coordinated escalation alongside the ongoing US naval blockade. Secondary-sanctions exposure now reaches into mainland Chinese refineries for the first time at scale, raising compliance pressure on Asian buyers of Iranian crude.

## Tags
ofac, iran, shadow-banking, shadow-fleet, china, secondary-sanctions, shahed-drones, ballistic-missiles, sanctions-evasion
